entry.title: Register Now: SAP Partner- Cert Certified Academies for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Q2- 2025)
entry.content:
For SAP Partners only 

We would like to invite you to the upcoming SAP enablement offering: Partner Get- Certified Academies for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

You will need a valid SAP Partner S User ID and a valid SAP Universal ID to access the links and register for the sessions

Dates : 18th June ; 23rd to 26th June

  • Option 1: Morning Session: 09:00 AM to 12:00 PM CET
  • Option 2: Evening Session: 04:00 PM to 07:00 PM CET

In Q2 edition of this event, we will run certification academies for the 4 GROW/ SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - certifications.

  • 18th June  - Implementation Consultant – Register (Mandatory to all LOB Certifications)
  • 23rd & 24th June - Financial Accounting Part 1 & Financial Accounting Part 2- Register
  • 25th June - Sourcing & Procurement – Register
  • 26th June - Sales – Register

Description:

Accelerate your timelines to get certified on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition -Certifications! Join this academy to obtain the certification guidance pertaining to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Certification - (SAP Certified Associate – Implementation Consultant) and Line of Business Certifications.

Learning Objective(s):

  • Allowing learners to build familiarity & understand certification guidance across various topics, covered in the SAP Learning Journeys, which reduces the time spent in the self-paced consumption of LJs.
  • Understanding of key concepts, tips & tricks to optimally consume the SAP Learning Journeys while preparing for SAP Certification exam.

Target Audience:

  • SAP S/4HANA - LoB Consultants ( and/or) SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Professionals looking to get certified on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Edition Certifications. 

Roles:

  • Architect, Consultant / Delivery, Developer, Project Manager, Technical Consultant

entry.title: How to cancel individual tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding without canceling the entire program
entry.content:
We've recently upgraded to ONB 2.0. There are some optional tasks in our onboarding and offboarding programs that were not completed and it doesn't make sense to have a manager complete them now that they are a couple of months old. They still show up on the manager homepage. 

How can we cancel or remove those individual, old tasks without canceling the entire program for those new hires and departing employees? We would like to make a regular process to clean up outdated and incomplete tasks. What would you recommend? Thanks!

entry.title: How to activate script server when express edition installed without xsa component
entry.content:
Daemon server and script server are not visible on SAP hana express edition (Hana 2.0 SPS7)  installation option without XSA as we are intended to use XS classic. Now we got stuck on data profiling operation by SDA connection as it needs Hana script server required to be running.

Error Msg:

"value": "InternalError: dberror(CallableStatement.execute): 256 - sql processing error: \"EHUB_PROFILING\".\"EHUB_PROFILING::SEMANTIC_PROFILING\": line 70 col 4 (at pos 2700): No ScriptServer available (see SAPNote 1650957)"
        }
    }

Installation Options:

##############################################################################
# Summary before execution #
##############################################################################
HANA, express edition installer : /home/sceis-admin/HANA_EXPRESS_20
Component(s) to install : HANA server, Application Function Library, SAP HANA EPM-MDS

Regards

Sandip

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Status Code Field Update
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil team has updated the Status Code (cStat) field, which now supports 5 digits. The supported electronic fiscal documents can process and interpret the new rejection codes returned by SEFAZ.

You can implement these changes by following the instructions on SAP Notes 3605421 and 3601578.

Take a look at the latest updates and features in our What's New session on the SAP Help Portal:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • Outbound Nota Fiscal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (Inbound invoicing option for Brazil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to determine whether a legal change has a planned solution. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, electronic document, electronic documents, eletronic document, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, eletronica, electronic nota fiscal, factura electrónica, conhecimento de transporte eletrônico, nf-e, ct-e, mdf-e, nf3-e, nfs-e, cstat, official status code, código de status da sefaz

após aplicação de várias notas SAP a J1BNFE está apresentando dump no momento do SET_NUMBER. O dump ocorre pois foi criada uma chamada no método FILL_CTE_400 na interface IF_EX_CL_NFE_PRINT.

Grato

entry.title: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part II
entry.content:
Disclaimer

These blogs aim to provide brief information with basics and fundamentals. If you want to investigate further, please visit Production Planning Optimization | SAP Help Portal

The approach is to give 1-fit-for-all as much as possible; mentioning details, processes, and personal recommendations that should be sufficient in most cases. You can take this as a starting point or a template, and then build own design.

 

Introduction

In PP/DS we have been introduced with a Production Planning Optimization (PPO) functionality for a while. In this blog series we are investigating this tool.

This blog is successor of "Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part I" (Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part I - SAP Community); if you want to know what PPO is, how different it is from MRP, what it offers, and when to address it, you can check the first blog.

In this second blog of the series, we will check:

  • PPO parameters on related master data
  • cost modeling of PPO
  • customizing for PPO

In next blogs, we will take a look at Automatic Cost Generation, PPO execution and evaluation.

 

PPO Related Master Data Fields

Material Master

  • Base Unit of Measure: Base unit of measure exists on all views of material master; but generally maintained on Basic Data 1 view, since it is not depending on any organizational unit. This unit of measure is determined by ERP modules, not determined by production planners; since it mainly affects inventory management and product costing. Bill of materials, lot sizes, cost/penalty parameters, etc. are all defined referencing this unit.
  • Procurement Type: Procurement Type parameter exists on MRP2 view of the material master. This is used by MRP or PPO, while determination of source of supply for order creation.

If procurement type is "E", planned order is created. PDS is used as source of supply.

If procurement type is "X", either planned order or stock transfer requisition or external purchase requisition can be created. PDS is used as source of supply of planned order, transportation lane is used as source of supply of stock transfer requisition. For external purchase requisition, there is no source of supply required.

If procurement type is "F", either stock transfer requisition or external purchase requisition can be created. Transportation lane is used as source of supply of stock transfer requisition. For external purchase requisition, there is no source of supply required.

According to procurement type, selection among available source of supplies is carried out based on related costs' and lead times' effect on total costs.

I recommend setting "No Order Without Source of Supply" parameter of Planning Version 000 as "E", within transaction /N/SAPAPO/MVM. In doing so, a planned order must have a PDS, no PDS no planned order for in-house production; on the other hand, vendor is not an obligation to create an external procurement requisition, so purchase requisitions can be created anonymously, sourcing to be determined later by purchasing department.

  • Planned Delivery Time: This parameter exists on MRP2 view of material master. It is used for defining lead time in case of external purchase requisition creation. Planned delivery time is defined in calendar days, so it is not depending on workdays or holidays defined in the system.
  • Production Storage Cost: This parameter exists on Advanced Planning view of material master. It is the cost of holding 1 base unit of stock for a calendar day, so it is per base unit per day.
  • Safety Stock Level: This parameter exists on MRP2 view of material master. It is additional constant requirement on stock to cover fluctuations in demand quantities or supply durations; thus, entire stock will not be available for all planned requirements, but some reserved for unplanned requirements. It is a quantity based on base unit of measure. 
  • Safety Time: This parameter exists on MRP2 view of material master. It is alternative to Safety Stock Level; but instead of maintaining a safety quantity, here a safety number of workdays is maintained. Thus, planned requirements are brought forward in time, so that if anything changes out of plan, there will still be some time to take action. It is not reserving stock, but it leads to early procurement against requirements to gain some time. It is in workdays, so holidays matter, based on production calendar defined in location master.

Technically, Safety Stock Level and Safety Time can be defined on a material at once; however I do not recommend using this approach. Either define Safety Stock Level or Safety Time for a specific material.

Safety Time does not result in excess stock, it just changes the perception of requirement date; thus, affects the bucket of a requirement.

  • Safety Stock Penalty: This parameter exists on Advanced Planning view of material master. It is the cost of having 1 base unit of available stock under safety stock level for a calendar day, so it is per base unit per day. If instead of safety stock level, safety time is defined; then this parameter is not utilized, because safety time does not refer to any stock level.
  • Procurement Cost: This parameter exists on Advanced Planning view of material master. If a material is available for external purchasing, this is the cost of creating an order as external purchase requisition for 1 unit, so it is per base unit.
  • Maximum Allowed Delay: This parameter exists on Advanced Planning view of material master, defined for Customer Demand (Sales Order) and Demand Forecast (Planned Independent Requirement) separately. It is the number of calendar days, which is checked by PPO, when a receipt covers a requirement in delay. If the delay exists and it is more than this number in days, then the receipt is not allowed; thus, leaving the requirement unfulfilled.

Mass maintenance of this parameter is also possible with /N/SAPAPO/PPO_DPEN transaction.

  • Delay Penalty: This parameter exists on Advanced Planning view of material master, defined for Customer Demand (Sales Order) and Demand Forecast (Planned Independent Requirement) separately. It is the cost of delaying 1 base unit of requirement for a calendar day, so it is per base unit per day.

Mass maintenance of this parameter is also possible with /N/SAPAPO/PPO_DPEN transaction.

  • Non-delivery Penalty: This parameter exists on Advanced Planning view of material master, defined for Customer Demand (Sales Order) and Demand Forecast (Planned Independent Requirement) separately. It is the cost of unfulfilling 1 base unit of requirement, so it is per base unit.

Mass maintenance of this parameter is also possible with /N/SAPAPO/PPO_DPEN transaction.

Resource

  • Relevant to Finite Scheduling Indicator: This parameter exists within each capacity header of a work center/resource master data. This indicator defines whether the related capacity can be overloaded or not. Actually, no capacity should be overloaded, of course, but some capacities are automatically constrained by other resources (bottleneck resources). If a capacity is not bottleneck, then this indicator should be blank.

For instance; there are consecutive operations on separate resources. The first resource can produce up to 1000 units/day, whereas second resource can produce up to 700 units/day. In this example, the second resource is set as finite and already avoids any production plan over 700 units for a day. Therefore; there is no need to mark the first resource as finite.

So, choose this setting of resources wisely, and only mark resources that define total production capacity of a factory as finite. The rest of the resources can be infinite, because they can never be allowed to overload anyway.

PDS

  • Discretize PDS Indicator: This parameter is defined for each PDS via PDS_MAINT transaction, at PDS header level. Mark it and discretize your PDSs.
  • Single Level Variable Production Cost: This parameter is defined for each PDS via PDS_MAINT transaction, at PDS header level. It defines the cost of creating an order as planned order for 1 unit, so it is per base unit.

Instead of maintaining these parameters for PDSs via PDS_MAINT transaction, there is a BAdI option to maintain them. CUSLNTRTO_ADDIN BAdI definition has method CHANGE_CIF_STRUCTURES, and within this method there is internal table CT_TRPROD. CT_TRPROD table has 3 fields related: SPRIO (PDS Priority), COST_VAR_S (Single Level Variable Production Cost), and PDISC (Discretize PDS).

Transportation Lane

  • Transportation Cost: This parameter is defined for means of transport on a transportation lane. It defines the cost of creating an order as stock transfer requisition for 1 unit for cost rate.
  • Unit for Cost Rate: Since transportation lanes serve several materials at once, each material can have separate base unit of measure. In order to have a consistent cost model, the transportation cost refers to this unit. This parameter is defined for means of transport on a transportation lane. Here, maintain the unit of measure that is the basis of entire cost model. Also make sure that all relevant materials have unit conversion from their base unit of measure to this unit of measure, unless this is not the base unit of measure itself.
  • Transportation Duration: This parameter is defined for means of transport on a transportation lane. It defines the lead time of related stock transfer requisition. It is defined in format of hhhhhh:mm. For instance; if the transportation duration is 24 hours, then it will be defined as 24:00.

 

Base PPO Cost Modeling

So far, as you may have noticed, some parameters are called "cost" and some are "penalty". Well, actually these 2 terms are synonymous. The difference is, doing something has costs, not doing something has penalty. After all, semantically they are the same thing. That is why we can call them as "cost" in general, at least I do 😊

Among all above mentioned parameters, below is the list of parameters we should consider during cost modeling:

  • Non-delivery Penalty on Material
  • Maximum Allowed Delay on Material
  • Delay Penalty on Material
  • Safety Stock Penalty on Material
  • Production Storage Cost on Material
  • Procurement Cost on Material
  • Single Level Variable Production (PDS) Cost on PDS
  • Transportation Cost on Means of Transport in Transportation Lane

Non-delivery Penalty

Non-delivery penalty is defined in billions.

You can prioritize finished goods. For example:

  • Class A finished goods' customer demand non-delivery penalty = 8.000.000.000
  • Class B finished goods' customer demand non-delivery penalty = 7.000.000.000
  • Class C finished goods' customer demand non-delivery penalty = 6.000.000.000
  • Class A finished goods' demand forecast non-delivery penalty = 3.000.000.000
  • Class B finished goods' demand forecast non-delivery penalty = 2.000.000.000
  • Class C finished goods' demand forecast non-delivery penalty = 1.000.000.000
  • All other materials' customer demand non-delivery penalty = 9.000.000.000
  • All other materials' demand forecast non-delivery penalty = 9.000.000.000

If no prioritization is required, non-delivery penalty can be set as 9.000.000.000 for all materials (including finished goods) both for customer demand and demand forecast.

Maximum Allowed Delay

It is the number of days, included within PPO horizon. It is the difference between last day of last bucket and first day of first bucket.

For example; if PPO buckets are weekly and PPO horizon is 3 weeks, then maximum allowed delay is 21. Or, if PPO buckets are monthly and PPO horizon is 6 months, then maximum allowed delay is 183.

Once maximum allowed delay is determined, maintain this value for all materials (including finished goods) both for customer demand and demand forecast.

Delay Penalty

Delay Penalty = Non-delivery Penalty / Maximum Allowed Delay - 1.

For example; if for a specific material, non-delivery penalty of customer demand is 6.000.000.000 and maximum allowed delay is 100, then delay penalty of customer demand for that material is 59.999.999

Safety Stock Penalty

Safety Stock Penalty = minimum{Delay Penalty of Demand Forecast, Delay Penalty of Customer Demand} / 2.

For example; if for a specific material, delay penalty of customer demand is 40.000.000 and delay penalty of demand forecast is 20.000.000, then safety stock penalty for that material is 10.000.000

Production Storage Cost

Production storage cost is defined in hundreds. Define it between 100 and 800.

First; determine location-product groups for which you want PPO to keep stock, in case there is excess stock left on hand due to lot sizing or fixed receipts or actual stocks. Then prioritize these groups.

Assign the lowest production storage cost (which is 100) to location-product group that you prefer to keep stock the most.

Then incrementally assign 200, 300, etc. to less prior groups respectively.

Finally assign the highest production storage cost (which is 800) to location-product group that you do not want to keep the stock at.

For example; you want to keep stock on raw materials in factory as much as possible. But if for some reason semi-finished goods are overproduced, you want to convert them into finished goods and keep the stock as finished goods. And let's say, keeping finished goods stocks in distribution center is better than keeping them in factory. Then maintain production storage costs as below:

  • Raw materials = 100
  • Semi-finished goods = 800
  • Finished goods at distribution center = 200
  • Finished goods at factory = 300

PDS, Transportation, and Procurement Costs

These 3 types of costs are used to select source of supply for a material. The rules about these costs depend on a material's procurement type

If the material procurement type is "E" (in-house): Transportation and Procurement cost is not required. Assign PDS costs based on priority; for example, assign PDS cost as 1 to preferred PDS, as 2 to second prior PDS, and so on.

If the material procurement type is "X" (in-house but external is also available): Assign PDS costs as PDS priorities (1, 2, 3, etc.). Then assign Transportation cost to transportation lanes (if exist) from 901 up to 908 based on priority. Finally assign Procurement cost as 909.

If the material procurement type is "F" (externally procured): Assign Transportation cost to transportation lanes (if exist) from 1 up to 8 based on priority. Finally assign Procurement cost as 9.

 

Normalization of PPO Cost Model

What is described above as generic cost model is the "base"; however, there are 2 arguments that need to be considered in order to have a "final" cost model:

  • Base Unit of Measures
  • BOM Quantity Ratios

These 2 arguments affect below costs/penalties:

  • Non-delivery Penalty
  • Delay Penalty
  • Safety Stock Penalty
  • Production Storage Cost

These 4 parameters need to be normalized according to mentioned arguments.

Unit of Measure Normalization

Let's say your raw materials and semi-finished goods are in kilograms (KG), but finished goods are in pieces (PC). As mentioned above, costs and penalties refer to base unit of measure. Therefore; for the sake of consistency all materials must have equivalent point of view. For example; kilogram equivalent of each finished goods may differ, such as, 250 grams/piece for finished good A, and 3 kilograms/piece for finished good B; which are produced from same semi-finished good C.

In this case, 1 unit cost of FG B is equivalent to 12 units cost of FG A, and 3 units cost of SFG C. Which means costs/penalties of FG A must be multiplied by 4, and costs/penalties of FG B must be divided by 3; in order to equalize them with SFG C, thus, maintaining the balance. This is called Unit of Measure Normalization.

After base cost modeling, select a cost model unit of measure (which is KG in this example), then convert base costs/penalties of 4 above-mentioned parameters accordingly before maintaining them on materials. Also make sure that all relevant materials have unit conversion from their base unit of measure to cost model unit of measure, unless it is not the base unit of measure itself.

BOM Ratio Normalization

If the cost model unit of measure has no dimension (such as piece, case, pallets, etc.), then there is another normalization to consider: BOM Ratio Normalization

If the cost model unit of measure has a dimension (such as weight, volume, etc.), then BOM Ratio Normalization is not required.

Let's say you are producing 1 piece (PC) of semi-finished good A, by consuming 2 pieces (PC) of semi-finished good B. This means, 1 unit of SFG A is equivalent to 2 units of SFG B.

In this case, costs/penalties of SFG B must be divided by 2, in order to equalize it with SFG A; thus, maintaining the balance. This is called BOM Ratio Normalization.

After base cost modeling, base costs/penalties of 4 above-mentioned parameters must be revised accordingly before maintaining them on materials.

 

Customizing PPO

In customizing of Advanced Planning, PPO related nodes can be found under SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Advanced Planning->Production Planning Optimization.

Optimization Server

Customizing node: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Advanced Planning->Production Planning Optimization->Optimizer Server Setup->Maintain Master Data for Optimization Server

Transaction: /N/SAPAPO/COPT00

Check PPO01 destination ID is active:

Check connection test button and see all lights are green in PPO01 row of popup window:

Optimization Profile

Customizing node: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Advanced Planning->Production Planning Optimization->Basic Settings->Define PP Optimizer Profiles

Transaction: /N/SAPAPO/PPO_PRFL

Choose "New Entries":

Name the profile and maintain a description, then choose "Maintain Profile":

Set header and General Constraints as seen in the screenshot:

Maintain horizon for Lot Sizes and Fixed Consumptions. In this example all are maintained as 4 weeks, which is last bucket of PPO horizon. For example; you may want to run PPO for 3 weeks, starting from next week; meaning the last bucket will be 4 weeks later than today:

Do not change the rest of the tabs. Leave them as they are.

Besides, make sure that all available cost multipliers are 1 within the Cost Multipliers tab:

Time Bucket Profile

Customizing node: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Advanced Planning->Production Planning Optimization->Basic Settings->Define Time Bucket Profiles

Choose "New Entries":

Name time profile and maintain description, then double click on Time Units folder:

Choose "New Entries":

Maintain bucket type and horizon for PPO. In this example it is maintained as 4 weeks, each starting on Mondays. Thinking of running PPO for 3 weeks, starting from next week; meaning the last bucket will be 4 weeks later than today:

Category Groups

Customizing node: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Advanced Planning->Production Planning Optimization->Basic Settings->Maintain Category Groups

SAP provides category groups from O01 to O07:

Each category group has their own list of categories. Please refer to OSS note for related categories: 2958477 - Setting Up Standard Category Profile for PP Optimizer - SAP for Me

Category Profile

Customizing node: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Advanced Planning->Production Planning Optimization->Basic Settings->Define Category Profiles

SAP provides a default category profile:

 

In this blog; we have covered PPO parameters, cost modeling, and PPO customizing. That is all for now. In the next blog we will investigate Automatic Cost Generation, where we don't have to worry about PPO parameters and cost modeling; thus, only PPO customizing will suffice, with some assumptions of course.

 

Previous Blog: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part I - SAP Community

entry.title: Request for Solution: CDS View with Parameters (Key Date) Not Supported in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently integrating a consumption CDS view with SAP Datasphere. This view includes parameters used for Key Date calculations. However, it appears that parameter-based CDS views are currently not supported in Datasphere, and as a result, these views are not visible or accessible within Datasphere.

Current Architecture:

  • Source: SAP S/4HANA CDS Views (with parameters)
  • Data Layer: SAP Datasphere
  • Cloud Storage: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  • Reporting Tool: Qlik Sense

Issue Description:

Due to the use of parameters (specifically for Key Date logic), the CDS views are not visible in Datasphere. To work around this, we attempted to create a wrapper CDS view to eliminate parameters by replacing the Key Date with the current system date ($systemDate). However, this approach does not yield the correct results. While it retrieves data until the current date, it fails to calculate key figures as expected based on Key Date logic.

Request for Guidance:

  1. Is there any supported approach in SAP Datasphere for handling CDS views with Key Date parameters?
  2. Are there alternative solutions or best practices in S/4HANA or Datasphere to handle such cases?
  3. Would it be possible to implement the Key Date logic in GCP?
    • If so, what changes or configurations would be required on the S/4HANA side to support this?
  5. Are there any recommended design patterns or tools to replicate the Key Date functionality in an environment where parameterized views are not supported?

This issue is critical and is currently impacting key project deliverables. Your urgent assistance would be highly appreciated.

Please find attached the CDS views in question, including those with parameters. Let us know if you need any additional details.

Thanks,

Sridhar

entry.title: Subsequent Activation of non-leading ledgers without history
entry.content:
This is related to ECC Classic Ledger to S/4 brown field migration and subsequent Parallel Ledger activation with different fiscal years.

Question: if we want to go with subsequent activation and don't want historical data in the non-leading ledgers, do you see any issues with going live with parallel ledgers in the same phase itself w/o having to go thru DMLT or any other complicated approach. Please note the fiscal calendars for these non-leading ledgers would be different. At a high level, the plan is to,

  1. Perform “in-place” upgrade of ECC to S/4 using the brown field approach
  2. Configure non-leading ledgers in S/4 and activate (no waiting for a year or so)
  3. Upload trial balances into the non-leading ledgers
  4. Release S/4 to business.

Please advise if you see any challenges with this. The one issue that we heard about is Fixed assets. Per SAP document, if we activate in the same year, then the depreciation entries cannot be portrayed by period. Everything will be consolidated under the current period. But I assume this would be for that just one year. For all subsequent years, the entries should look properly. Are there any issues that you foresee?

entry.title: Best Practices for Removing Firefighter ID Owner in SAP GRC
entry.content:
We encountered an issue where a Firefighter ID was assigned to an owner and could not be deleted. We successfully resolved the issue by removing entries from NWBC → Setup → Emergency Access Assignment → Firefighter IDs.

While we have identified the solution, we would like to ensure that we are following SAP-recommended best practices to avoid any unintended consequences, such as:

  • Impact on historical logs and audit trails

  • Potential system inconsistencies or dependencies

  • Recommended validation steps before deletion

Request:

Could you provide SAP"s official best practices for safely removing Firefighter ID assignments while maintaining compliance and system integrity? If there are specific SAP Notes or guidelines, please share them.

System Details:

  • SAP GRC Version: AC FOR S/4HANA V8100 SP 17 (06/2022)

entry.title: Which Synchronous Posting is enabled from EWM to S4 & relevant Service Class for posting.
entry.content:
Synopsis: Understanding which of the business processes are activated as 'Synchronous Posting' from EWM to S4. There is lot of stuff available in SAP community related to synchronous posting from S4 to EWM( Inbound External Receipts and Inbound Internal Receipt( MO), primarily a MIGO capability However, there are even direct synchronous posting conducted from EWM2S4. 

Requirement: Synchronous posting from EWM2S4 are generally required because of following reasons. 

  • Avoid hanging queue from EWM2S4 
  • Avoid stock inconsistencies between EWM & S4
  • Real time storage location extension during posting from /SCWM/ADGI( GM code=04, NWLO from EWM to non-EWM storage location).    

Logic of Synchronous Posting from EWM2S4: Activation at warehouse level( SPRO-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Interfaces-->Goods Movements-->Activate Synchronous Goods Movement Posting)

/SCWM/CL_ERP_GOODSMVT_SYNC_S4 is the maintain service class for synchronous posting from EWM2S4.

Two important methods play a pivotal role for synchronous posting

/SCWM/IF_ERP_GOODSMVT_SYNC~SIMULATE_POSTING--> This method checks the corresponding S4 Data like sufficient stock availability or even master data extension like 'material is extended to storage location or nor in real time to avoid hanging queues and display of error at 'UI Level' at 10 EWM relevant transaction.

IS_GM_CODE_ALLOWED: This is the main method displaying the EWM relevant transaction meant for Synchronous posting from EWM to S4. There are total relevant processes, the blog will highlight the /SCWM/ADGI synchronous capability only from EWM to S4.

 

  Below example is moving a Material from EWM managed to IM managed storage location using /SCWM/ADGI, GM Code=4)

System does the real time storage location extension check instead of posting and creating a hanging queue( If No Auto Sloc creation is enable in 411 Mvt type in S4). 

The class /SCWM/CL_ERP_GOODSMVT_SYNC_S4 with method /SCWM/IF_ERP_GOODSMVT_SYNC~SIMULATE_POSTING performs all relevant synchronous posting simulation before posting goods movement. In below example, a real time stock availability check is performed during a Material movement from EWM to IM managed storage location.

Similarly all relevant synchronous posting can be conducted from EWM2S4 with below transaction as well which are mentioned 

  • Difference analyzer(/SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER)
  • Difference analyzer(/DIFF_ANA_CAT_CHG)- Stock category change during posting.
  •  Initial Stock upload( /SCWM/ISU)
  • Posting change ( /SCWM/POST ) 
  • /Unplanned Goods Issue( /SCWM/RGI_SCRAP)- /SCWM/ADGI
  • Stock type change report( R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE )
  • monitor posting change via 'BBD/SLED Overview'--->MON_DDATES_PC', 
  • monitor posting change via 'Physical Stock--> 'MON_PHYSTO_PC',
  • Consumption against PMR- 'MFG_GI', 
  • Goods Movement Kanban Staging task Wbse to PSA- TO_KANBAN', 

Thanks for reading. All relevant information replicated is based on my personal experience and insight. Relevant tests have been performed in S/4 HANA 2023 FPS02 system.

entry.title: How to schedule background job with specific logic in SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Overview

In lob systems, business needs to run some repeative background job periodically like in financial closing time monthly. In ECC or SAP S/4HANA Cloud.An ABAP Developer can develop an classic ABAP program and schedule background job with transaction code SM36. But to keep core clean, classic ABAP should be avoided and reduced. So our customers are checking how to realize the similar functions SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Today, I will demo the step by step processes .

Prerequisites.

  1. You finished the BTP ABAP Environment initial setup .
  2. You have been assigned with role SAP_BR_DEVELOPER in BTP ABAP Environment.
  3. You have been assigned with role SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR in BTP ABAP Environment.
  4. You have installed Eclipse with ADT .

 

Process.

  • Step 1. Create ABAP class with interface if_apj_rt_run in Eclipse.

 

In next screen, select the transport request and Finish.

Replace the class code as the following ABAP codes:

CLASS zcl_application_job DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_run .
    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_defaults.

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_name_range,
        sign   TYPE c LENGTH 1,
        option TYPE c LENGTH 2,
        low    TYPE c LENGTH 50,
        high   TYPE c LENGTH 50,
      END OF ty_name_range.
    TYPES: ty_name_ranges TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_name_range WITH EMPTY KEY.

    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Numbers available</p>
    DATA numbers_available TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_true.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Numbers</p>
    DATA numbers TYPE RANGE OF i.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Names available</p>
    DATA names_available TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_true.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Names</p>
    DATA names TYPE ty_name_ranges.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Some text</p>
    DATA text TYPE c LENGTH 255.
    "! <p class="shorttext synchronized" lang="en">Public attribute which is not used on the selection screen</p>
    DATA another_public_attribute TYPE c LENGTH 10.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_application_job IMPLEMENTATION.


  METHOD if_apj_rt_run~execute.

    TRY.
        DATA(l_log) = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(
                        header = cl_bali_header_setter=>create( object = 'ZOBJECT'
                                                                subobject = 'ZSUBOBJECT' ) ).

        IF numbers_available = abap_true AND '42' IN numbers.
          l_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_information
                                                                    text = '42 is in the number ranges' ) ).
        ENDIF.

        IF names_available = abap_true AND names IS NOT INITIAL.
          l_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_status
                                                                    text = 'Some names are available' ) ).
        ENDIF.

        l_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create( severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_status
                                                                  text = CONV #( text ) ) ).

        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log_2nd_db_connection( log = l_log
                                                                     assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).
      CATCH cx_bali_runtime INTO DATA(l_runtime_exception).
        " some error handling
    ENDTRY.


  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Save and Activate the class.

  • Step 2. Create Application Job Catalog Entry in Eclipse.

 

 

 

In next screen, select the transport request and finish .

Save and Activate.

 

  • Step 3. Create Application Job Template in Eclipse.

 

 

 

In next screen, select the transport request and finish .

 

Save and Activate.

 

  • Step 4. Schedule the background job in BTP ABAP Environment .

 

 

 

 

 

 

The End!

Thank you for your time!

Jacky Liu

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Connect to Microsoft 365 Mail with OAuth2
entry.content:
The Error Message "The resource principal named https://outlook.office.com/IMAP.AccessAsUser.Allhttps://outlook.office.com was not found in the tenant named <some GOID>"

indicates that you have not separated the different scope names in the "Scope" field by space; instead of space you used a new line (CRLF) for separating the scopes.

In the documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/deploying-oauth2-client-authorization-code-with-microsoft-365-as-provider is mentioned: "If you add more than one scope, you need to separate your scopes by a blank space."

entry.title: Re: Manage Posting Periods - CDS View call
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

To create an arrangement or CDS view that exposes or replicates the functionality of the FAC_GL_MAINT_POSTING_PERIOD_SRV/PostingPeriodSet OData service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you cannot directly enhance or change standard OData services. However, you can create a custom CDS view that reads from the same underlying tables or views used by this standard service and expose it as your own custom OData service.

Here’s a step-by-step outline to achieve this:

 

Step 1: Understand the Source of the Standard OData Service

The standard OData service FAC_GL_MAINT_POSTING_PERIOD_SRV uses the entity set PostingPeriodSet, which most likely reads from tables such as:

  • FAGLBSAS – G/L Account Balances (less likely)
  • FINSC_PERIOD – (most likely, contains period status per ledger/company code/fiscal year)
  • V_T001B – Company code-specific posting period data
  • T001B – Posting period variant
  • T001 – Company code master
  • T009 – Fiscal year variant

Since you can’t modify the standard service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), your best bet is to model a CDS view on top of these tables or standard views.

 

Step 2: Create a Custom CDS View in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Open the Custom CDS Views app (F1866).
  2. Click Create.
  3. Provide a name like ZC_POSTING_PERIOD_OVERVIEW.
  4. Choose Scenario: Standard CDS View.
  5. Primary Data Source:
    • Try to use I_PostingPeriod, if available (this is the interface CDS view for posting periods).
    • Otherwise, create a view using table FINSC_PERIOD.
  6. Add fields such as:
    • Company Code
    • Ledger
    • Fiscal Year
    • Period
    • Period Start/End Dates
    • Period Status (Open/Closed)
    • Posting Period Variant (from T001)
    • User Role (if needed for logic)
  7. Add associations (e.g. to Company Code or Fiscal Year Variant) if needed for filtering or joins.
  8. Add Parameters (optional): For filtering by fiscal year, ledger, or company code.

 

Step 3: Publish Your CDS View as an OData Service

  1. In the same Custom CDS View app:
    • Go to the “Service Definition” tab.
    • Check OData Service.
    • Save and publish the view.
  2. After activation, your CDS view is exposed as an OData service automatically.

 

Step 4: Consume Your Custom OData Service

The external system can now access the CDS-based service using the endpoint:

/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_POSTING_PERIOD_OVERVIEW_CDS/

Where ZC_POSTING_PERIOD_OVERVIEW_CDS is the technical name of the published CDS view.

You can query this service like:

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_POSTING_PERIOD_OVERVIEW_CDS/EntitySetName?$filter=CompanyCode eq '1000' and FiscalYear eq '2025'

 

Limitations to be aware of (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

  • You cannot use ABAP code (no custom logic in CDS).
  • You cannot enhance standard OData services directly.
  • CDS views are read-only, not suitable for updates.
  • You must stick to whitelisted (released) views and tables.

 

Optional: explore released views in View Browser

Use the View Browser app (F2170) to find released CDS views like:

  • I_PostingPeriod
  • C_PostingPeriodVariant
  • I_FiscalYearVariant
  • I_CompanyCode

 

I hope those elements will help you find a solution.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Create Advanced Return on Sales/Purchase Tax without...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, when working with Advanced Returns Management (ARM) and Sales/Purchase Tax handling, the system typically requires a profit center because:

  • Profit centers are mandatory for certain accounting postings due to universal journal (ACDOCA) architecture.
  • Advanced Returns often involve inventory movements, value adjustments, or credit/debit memos, which trigger accounting documents needing profit center assignment for proper CO reporting and reconciliation.

So to make it short :

No, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition requires a profit center for financial postings, including advanced returns involving tax impacts.

 

I hope this will help you understand the logic behind it.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Restrict the restricted user status batches for speci...
entry.content:
To enforce a strict prohibition of "restricted-use" batches from entering specific storage types, custom logic might be necessary.

EWM provides BAdIs that allow for such detailed control.
 `/SCWM/EX_CORE_PTS_FILT_SORT` or `/SCWM/EX_CORE_PTS_MIX`  could be implemented. These BAdIs are called during the putaway bin determination process and can be used to filter or disallow specific storage bins (or even entire storage types) based on characteristics of the incoming product, including its batch status. You could, for instance, prevent the system from proposing a specific storage type if the incoming WT is for a restricted batch.

The BAdI `/SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_STOCK` for stock checks during goods movements could also be leveraged to define additional restrictions or trigger an error message if a movement of a restricted batch into a non-permitted storage type is attempted.

entry.title: Re: Turn off e-mail notification for specific users
entry.content:
Hello @EChou06 ,

You can Block all emails triggered from Learning to specific email address.

To do this, Navigate to  System Administration> Configuration> Global Application Settings > Mail tab.

Enter the emails under 'Block all emails to'.

This will block all the emails to the added email addresses generated from LMS.

If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Swapnil

Requirement:
We need to update the  Total Quantity  in the Production Order (see screenshot 2), using the value from the  Total Weight  field in our custom RAP screen (screenshot 1).

📎 Attached Screenshots:
- Screenshot 1: RAP Custom Screen (shows Total Weight = 140.000)
- Screenshot 2: Production Order screen (where we want to update the quantity)

What We Tried:
We are using this CDS view:  I_ProductionOrdConfirmationTP
2. Entity:  ProductionOrderConfirmation
3. Field: ConfirmationYieldQuantity

We are triggering the confirmation logic, but the quantity does not reflect in the production order.

Issue:

2. Do we need to use a different service or BAPI for this update in the Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: How to restrict Keyboard shortcuts using screen perso...
entry.content:
Yes, based on this blog post you can do that. Just replace the VKEY and VCOMP values according to your needs (3 and 11) and use return true instead of return.

In addition, other than the caller referencing the button presses, you also need to address the possibility of using the context menu options for the actions you want to suppress. So, for instance, 'Back' is possible in three ways: Keyboard, pressing the 'Back' button on the screen and using the 'Back' context menu item. The latter can be handled by adding the corresponding option to the branch with session.findById("wnd[0]").EVENT_VCOMP
Alternatively, you could disable those context menu options via an onLoad script. This command will hide the fourth context menu item, since numbering starts at 0:

session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/mnu/menu[3]").hide();

Keep in mind though, that Personas is not a security solution and if the user disables it via the appropriate URL parameter, they can access the Original Screen and avoid your logic.

entry.title: Product Compliance SVT: Error "You cannot delete this legal entity as it is used in mappings."
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Product Compliance SVT, I had created a Legal Entity Mapping incorrectly and wanted to delete it. Since there is no delete option in "Manage Legal Entity Mappings" screen, I tried to use the app "Manage Legal Entities - Product Compliance" and opened the Legal Entity that had the incorrectly mapped Compliance Requirement. There is a "Delete" button there but when I try to use the Delete, I see the error message "You cannot delete this legal entity as it is used in mappings.". I found that I could change the Valid to date in the Edit mode to a past date but the incorrectly mapped Compliance Requirement entry still remains in the list (though the Edit button is no longer seen once the validity date is changed and saved). Is there any way to delete the incorrect Compliance Requirement from the Legal Entity mapping list. 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP S4HANA Columbus Q2 meetup
entry.content:
Join us for the SAP S/4HANA Columbus Q2 Meetup, an exciting opportunity to connect with industry professionals, share insights, and explore the latest developments in SAP S/4HANA. This event is designed for SAP enthusiasts, IT professionals, and business leaders looking to stay ahead in the world of enterprise resource planning.

 

What to Expect

This meetup will feature engaging discussions on the latest updates, trends, and best practices in SAP S/4HANA. Our agenda includes:

  • Keynote Speakers: Hear from leading SAP experts and thought leaders as they share their insights on the evolving capabilities of SAP S/4HANA, including advancements in cloud, AI, and analytics.

  • Panel Discussions: Participate in interactive sessions covering real-world implementations, challenges, and success stories from organizations leveraging SAP S/4HANA.

  • Networking Opportunities: Connect with peers, exchange ideas, and build valuable relationships within the SAP community in Columbus.

Why Attend?

  • Stay updated on the latest SAP S/4HANA features and roadmap.

  • Learn from real-world case studies and expert insights.

  • Network with local SAP professionals and grow your professional circle.

  • Discover strategies to maximize the value of your SAP investments

Car Parking

There is visitor car parking available outside the building. No need to pay any parking fee.

Food

Food and soft drinks will be served. (Sponsored by - eBusiness Solutions Inc.)

Room no

Meeting Room no. 4 will be used for this meetup.

entry.title: Issue with Screen Enhancement PBO in SAP AS03 (Asset Master) - On-Premise 2020 vs. 2023
entry.content:
Dear Team/Colleagues,

I am encountering an issue with a screen enhancement implemented for the Asset Master Data (transactions AS01, AS02, AS03) in our SAP on-premise 2020 system.

I followed the steps outlined in the SAP Community blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/adding-z-fields-to-asset-master-screen-aist0002-t-codes-as01-as02-as03/ba-p/13208878 to add custom Z-fields.

The problem is that the Process Before Output (PBO) module of my custom subscreen is not being triggered in our SAP on-premise 2020 release. As a result, I am unable to disable fields in AS03, and any validations or Listbox functionalities defined in the PBO are not working.

Interestingly, I've observed that the PBO is being triggered correctly for the same enhancement in an SAP on-premise 2023 release system.

Could you please help me understand why this behaviour differs between the two releases and what I might have missed or need to adjust for our 2020 environment?

Thank you

entry.title: PCo CONFIGURATIONS
entry.content:
Note: My previous account was marked as a former member(former_member532862). As a result, I am re-posting my blogs here for continued reference and visibility.


PCo:


PCo is known as Plant Connectivity. This tool is used to connect different plant applications. SAP MII has a dedicated PCo connector  to communicate with PCo.

In this document we will cover



  • Save file



  • Read file



  • OPC connection

 


PCo Panels:

 



Save file via PCo:


Steps:



  • Create a folder to save file

 

 

 



 



  • Create a source system to save file the folder

 

 

 





  • Create an agent using the source system



    • Enable the checkbox Run Agent Instance as an Executable

 

 

 

 

 









    • Configure the Query Ports

 

 

 

 

 





  • Create the PCo connector at MII

 

 

 





  • Use PCo query to send the file

 

 

 



Tag store Query

 



Execute the query

 



Result:



  • File created at the source folder

 

 

 



Read File Via PCo:





  • Create a File monitoring source system to read the folder

 

 

 





  • Create an agent using the File monitoring source system



    • Subscribe File Contain and File Name

 

 

 

 

 



Create a notification

 



Subscribe the output elements

 



Select a destination transaction

 





  • Result:The destination Transaction gets executed on creation of a new file in the folder.

Connect OPC Via PCo – Write Values in tag:





    • Create a OPC DA source system to connect KEP Wear

 

 

 

 

 



 

Connect OPC Via PCo – Send OPC Notification:





  • Create a OPC DA source system to connect KEP Wear

 

 

 



Subscribe the tags needed

 



Create a notification and add a trigger condition

 



Add the output elements

 



Select a destination transaction

 



 

Result:

On change on the values of the tag, the destination transition gets executed.

 

 

Note: When we create an agent, it creates an windows service.

 



OPC connection from MII





  • Create a OPC DA source system for MII

 


 

 



Configure the query Ports

 



Create a PCo connection in MII. * Shown above.

Create a PCo query to send data to OPC tag.

MII agent and windows Services


“Run agent instance as an executable” – In most of the implementations, we enabled this checkbox when we needed the agent to be used from an outside system like SAP MII. We also differentiated the agents by two categories:



  1. Agents will subscribe items and will send notification



  1. Agents will only work as a connector for the MII system

 


The agents which subscribe and notifies MII are always needed to be running to keep reading the subscribed items. Whereas for the 2nd type of agents do not have any subscription item and will only listen to the port for any incoming request. So, the second type of agents does not require to run as a service always.

We realized when we enable the “Run agent instance as an executable”, the service is in stopped condition but still working as desired.

Example Screenshots below:

 



 

 



 

 

 



 

After categorizing the agents as above, we saw the server resource utilization decreased comparing before when we used to create the agents by disabling “Run agent instance as an executable”.

entry.title: Re: I want to create product code is 01010004BK when crea...
entry.content:
Hi @Wai 

Number ranges like 01010004BK wont be possible with internal assignment. 

Normally alphanumeric numbers should be external assignment but in some cases where number ranges are like A000000000 - A999999999, alphanumeric is possible with external assignment but in your case alphanumeric is not in sequence, so it wont be possible to assign internal number assignment.

I hope it clears your doubt. Check n confirm

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: CALL TRANSFORMATION - CX_ST_MATCH_ELEMENT exception
entry.content: Said shortly, I can't reproduce your problem because you don't post a reproducible example: it's missing the full XML, the full transformation (and call transformation, but usually it's not the culprit). Personally, when I ask a question about something which doesn't work, I create a minimal program which can be executed by anyone who can then execute it and reproduce the problem, debug it and fix it (logic of Stack Overflow "minimal reproducible example").

entry.title: Re: Issue with Screen Enhancement PBO in SAP AS03 (Asset Master) - On-Premise 2020 vs. 2023
entry.content: 1) If your custom fields are displayed, then the PBO is automatically executed, so it would be interesting to see what your PBO looks like, which module is not executed, and you should also confirm whether an unconditional module at the top of the PBO is executed. 2) Check the SAP notes (but I doubt there's a bug).

entry.title: How to Design Production Processes for Pre-Complete Assembly Check of Process Manufacturing in SAP D
entry.content:
Note: My previous account was marked as a former member(former_member532862). As a result, I am re-posting my blogs here for continued reference and visibility.

 

How to Design Production Processes for Pre-Complete Assembly Check of Process Manufacturing in SAP DM

Design Production Processes
To design production processes without any coding skills DMC delivers the app Design Production Processes. The app helps to model various production processes using a graphical design tool. The production processes define the interaction between machines or define rules, actions, and workflows that control the execution on the shop floor. The custom-made business process extension can be consumed and used like a standard business service within the Design Production Process app.

Objective: Define the production process for per-complete SFC check. Hence the process should be by clicking on the Complete button production process will be triggered and check if the user assembled or not. If the components are assembled, then the system allow to complete the SFC otherwise the system doesn’t allow to complete the SFC.
Step:1 Create Production Process
Need to go Design Production Processes app (Path->Manufacturing Automation->Design Production Processes), now able to create a production process. Here, we will learn how to create a production process design for a pre-complete assembly check (Process Manufacturing). Need to put the Production process name and description and create the process.

Need to put Name, ID, Description, and Runtime Type to create production process, now process variable session is enabled to declare production process. Now go to the editor and design the production process as per the requirement.

Step:2 Production Process flow
At 1st take the start controls and need to add the Inputs parameters as per the requirement by clicking on the manage parameters button. After that need to add Check Component services and map the input parameter as per the requirement.

As we know for process manufacturing GI is being confirmed against phase. Hence, need to map the Operation as Phase

Now, need to add the Complete SFC service and map the Body parameter as per the requirement.

Now, need to end the production process. Hence to need the End controls.

Step:3 Test the Production Process
Need to save the Production Process by clicking on the Save All button, click on the Quick Deploy to deploy the created production process, and run the process standalone.

Click on the Run button to execute the Production process standalone. Then put the required input and again click on the Run button.


Step:4 Add the Production Process into the Complete Button
Need to go to the POD designer (Path->Manufacturing Configuration->POD Designer), and open the custom POD (Process Order).

Now, need to configure the Complete button. Go to assign action and add the created production process. Also, need to map the variable with parameters.



Now, it has been working through the POD.


Thanks,
Rajat

entry.title: Re: Connection issue in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: RFC_INVALID_HANDLE cannot be the cause of the problem, it is a consequence of the problem. It means, that at some earlier point, a connection broke down (or was closed deliberately by DataSphere), but then DataSphere keeps trying to use this no longer existing connection. So you should check for some other (perhaps network related) error preceding this one. Or perhaps turn on RFC trace and check what's going on.

entry.title: XSteps (Execution Steps) Configuration for two different destinations (external system)
entry.content:
Spoiler
Note: My previous account was marked as a former member(former_member532862). As a result, I am re-posting my blogs here for continued reference and visibility.

1. Overview:

  • DefinitionA structure for describing the precise execution of production operations. We can regulate the data interaction with the process control level and generate interactive work instructions/PI sheets for process operators and production workers using XSteps. A master recipe, process order, standard routing, or production order can all be allocated an XStep.
  • Advantages of using Xstep
    a. Perfect building blocks for standardized procedures in production sites all over the globe.
    b. XML export and import of complete XStep repositories and SXS elements.
    c. Usage of XSteps in Process orders and Master Recipe.
    d. No PI Sheet simulation capabilities in process orders that use classical process instructions.
    e. XStep allows multiple usages of phase context in combination with multiple control recipe destinations.
    f. Xsteps is easy to maintain and lead time to change or maintain the Xsteps.
  • Structure


An XStep tree is made up of one or more XSteps. It may also include XStep process instructions and references to standard XSteps (SXS reference). In an XStep tree, an XStep can be composed of other XSteps, SXS references, and XStep process instructions.
We can create the following components within an XStep tree.

  • XSteps
    An XStep can have additional subordinated XSteps. Higher-level XStep properties such as scope of generation, control instruction/control recipe destinations, and application context are inherited by subordinate XSteps if they have not been expressly allocated any of these values individually. If, for example, the application context Order is defined in the super ordinate XStep, it is valid for all subordinate XSteps until the next XStep for which a different context is established.
  • SXS References
    An XStep or a standard XStep can refer to variations of a standard XStep that are kept in the SXS repository. When a SXS reference is created, the system first establishes a loose link to the referenced standard XStep. The system produces a duplicate of the valid version of the standard XStep in your XStep tree for the key date after the references are exploded.
  • XStep Process Instructions
    All functions that were available with the conventional process instructions are still available. It is no longer necessary to be familiar with the syntax of the process instruction characteristics while maintaining objects in the XStep editor. Instead, we may use the context menu to easily establish output and input fields, as well as control tasks like function calls and process messages.

    2. Objective/Case Study
    Previously, the User had one external system (SAP MII) and they used to send all the information to the external system (SAP MII) through “Xsteps” for execution purposes. Recently, they have introduced/implemented a new external system for barcode printing. From now onwards need to send all information for both the system as well. So, need to configure a new Xstep under the master recipe for barcode printing system.
  • Assigning XSteps to MII Master Recipes
    Note:The below steps describe how to add the xsteps necessary for the operation/phase execution (destination D1/Q1/P1). Needed on all Finished Goods.
    Finally, we need the xsteps to drive the Barcode Printer Integration on all Finished. (Destination D3/Q3/P3).
    Here is what the setup should look like for each type:
  • Operation/Phase and Barcode Printer Needed (all finished goods):


  • Detailed Instructions for Barcode Printer (P3 Destination):

Step1:
Goto tr. C202 put the recipe and change number to assign XSteps and enter.


Step 2:
Click on the xstep icon.

Step 3:
The Xstep page will be opened and click on Xstep tree.


Step 4:
Double-click on xstep tree icon and a pop-up window will be displayed, select the “Destination” tab.
Step 5:
Expand “External systems” and double click “Kitchen - MES” – P3, please wait until that appears in the Current Assignment box. And Close the popup by clicking “x”.

Step 7:
Right-click on the xstep tree icon and click “SXS Reference”. SXS Reference will be created.


Step 8:
Double-click on the “New SXS Reference” and a pop-up will be displayed. Select the “context” tab.
Double-click on the header, and wait until the assignment is copied in the Current assignment box.
Step 9:
Select the “Reference tab” from the pop-up window. Drill down through the folder until you get the “All Phases” reference xstep. Double-click “All Phases” and wait until it appears on the current assignment box.
Step 10:
Select the “General” tab from the popup. Mention “All Phases” in the description box. And close the popup by clicking “x”.

Step 11:
Right-click on the xstep tree icon and click “SXS Reference” and a popup will be displayed. Select the “Context” tab.
Double-click on the “Good Receipt”, and wait until the assignment is copied in the Current assignment box.
Step 12:
Select the “Reference tab” from the pop-up window. Drill down through the folder until you get the “GR Phase” reference xstep. Double-click “GR Phase” and wait until it appears on the current assignment box.

Important Note: for destination D1/Q1P1 we specify the Bottleneck Phase. But for the Barcode Printer Integration (Destination D3/Q3/P3) it is the GR Phase that is called out.  Make sure to choose the correct item and link it to the correct phase. This is how MII knows which phase to use for Goods Receipt or the Bottleneck Phase Confirmation.
Step 13:
Select the “General” tab from the popup. Mention “GR Phase” on the description box. And close the popup by clicking “x”.

Step 13:
Right-click on “GR Phase” and select the “Explode” and “GR Phase” mentioned as exploded. New xstep and SXS Reference has been generated.


Step 14:
Also, need to add another “SXS Reference” by right click on the xstep tree icon and clicking “SXS Reference” and a popup will be displayed. Select the “Reference” tab.

Double-click on the “Bottleneck Phase”, and wait until the assignment is copied into the Current assignment box.
Step 15:
Right click the xstep tree and click on “check”. “No errors were found” will be displayed in the information bar.
Step 16:
Xstep structure should look like this for all Kitchen-MES lines.

Step 17: 
Save the Master recipe.

  • Conclusion
    After the above Xsteps configuration, able to send all the required information to both external systems.


Thanks,
Rajat

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
@nageshcaparthy 

Thank you for the great blog—it was very useful. However, I found an issue in section 8.3: Running the Jobs.

The job completes with "Skipped Users" during the "WRITE" action on the target system. Upon investigation, I discovered that the users are being skipped due to the absence of a Global User ID in the source user data from the S/4HANA system.

In the transformation JSON, the following condition is present, which requires both the emails and userUuid (Global User ID) fields to be non-empty:

"condition": "($.emails EMPTY false) && ($['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User'].userUuid EMPTY false)"

I verified in SU01 on the S/4HANA system that the affected users do not have an External User ID (UID) or Global User ID, as they were created directly in the system.

Have you encountered a similar issue? If so, could you kindly share any guidance or recommended steps to resolve it?

Thank and Appreciate

entry.title: Enterprise Architecture in the time of VUCA
entry.content:
What is VUCA?

VUCA[1] stands for Volatile, Uncertain, Complex and Ambiguous.

It’s not a new acronym. Actually, it was first used to describe post-Cold War challenges back in 1987, but its popularity in Business terminology (in describing “disruptive” and “complex” environment) came only with new millennium.

From the beginning to…

But before we go into “World of VUCA”, lets start with the Enterprise Architecture “beginnings”.

Actually, I do not want to go into “full” history of Enterprise Architecture, there are plenty of articles[2] covering it in details… To make the long story short – the need to “architect” the Enterprise and align Business needs with IT Capabilities, came with the grooving dependency of all businesses form the emerging IT presence in every Enterprise. Let’s just say, as a discipline, Enterprise Architecture started with John Zachman and Zachman Framework[3] in the late 1980s, and continued with DODAF[4] in the early 1990s. Many would say (incl. me) traditional Enterprise Architecture started reaching its full maturity with TOGAF[5] as of 1995. It looked like, sky was a limit…

Well, while Enterprise Architecture as a discipline was gaining (rightfully) the momentum in the coming years, some “disruptions” were at sight…

  • First, and probably the most important; the pace of changes in the World (around us) was (and still is) growing in the “logarithmic” curve – “things” started changing too fast.
  • Second, contributing significantly, Enterprise Architecture has been raising very high (unrealistic?) expectations about bringing immediate Business Value.

So, this is where VUCA kicks-in[6]

Clash with reality…

Traditional Enterprise Architecture like TOGAF framework with ADM (Architecture Development Method) methodology is simply too ridged and too slow to adjust to VUCA… And yes, VUCA is all around us. Do we know how much time it takes to go through the full ADM cycle? Do we know how many “strict” artifacts ADM needs to produce? How, and how fast, are those “tons” of artifacts in really used by those teams who need to operationalize the Architectural Vision and deliver the actual Business Value?

Yes, there are organizations which could afford (at least for some time?) more strict and more rigid use of the Enterprise Architecture, but in reality, many organizations had to be very agile to maintain their operational sustainability.

This was inevitably leading to a decline of interest in the Enterprise Architecture discipline.

But wait…

This was not the end. In fact, this “scream” for more lean approaches to the Enterprise Architecture, was redefining Enterprise Architecture into more “grounded” discipline, more “connected” with the agile Business. This resulted in tailoring and adopting Enterprise Architecture to be “closer” with real life Business scenarios – or simply said, to be more applicable in the wider number of organizations. To name few flavors:

  • Architectural Thinking[7]
  • Lean EA by AppHaus[8]
  • SAP EAF (SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework)[9] – also brining a lot form AppHaus, but keeping most of the TOGAF as well
  • …

Those more into agile manifesto, would go even further in rebuilding EA role completely – like in SAFe[10]. However, SAFe is not an “adaptation” of TOGAF, but fully fledged framework on its own.

And now (for the last few years) we are witnessing “re-birth” of EA profession. But this time EA, or Architecture practice in general, is much closer to its agile Business. Why? The need to “architect” the Enterprise and align Business needs with IT never stopped… The need is even higher, but these alignments had to be lean and agile enough… Now, “re-born” Enterprise Architecture is more “equipped” and more “prepared” to keep the track with VUCA…

Now, we are now embracing the fact, there is no “real” TO-BE state.

We are working with VUCA…

EA is about Capabilities… EA is about Principles…

Yes, it is. And this must stay the solid truth of EA work. These two are fundamental parts of the strategic Architectural Vision (to be aligned with Business) and Architectural Guardrails (how to build the solutions).

But Architecture, in general, is about resolving (or solutioning) the problems – i.e. staring with Solution Context, through Architecture Vision (or Solution Concept) to GAP analysis, and finally building the TO-BE state. Of course, I am mentioning only few “key” phases – and each may bring several distinct artifacts. Additional phases (like Stakeholder map etc.) for sure can be included – but not need for each initiative, especially when build inhouse solution roadmaps…

The agility (and I do not emphasize on any specific framework or methodology) is teaching us – there is no more “final” TO-BE state… We are at Continues Transformation[11][12], just as we are at Continues Implementation, Continues Delivery (CI/CD) cycles. And this is a fundamental change in the mindset of an Architect – no matter if he/she is embracing more lean approach to EA (i.e. through various adaptations of TOGAF), or he/she is just following agile manifesto (i.e. like SAFe)…

Conclusions

So, which Enterprise Architecture framework/methodology should we apply, these days?

It’s all about – getting things done… I would say, use common sense approach.

I would never go for rigid approach with all artifacts (even in past when TOGAF was “ruling”)… But, TOGAF is still the “essence” of the Enterprise Architecture – we just needed to tailor it to fit current needs (in the World of VUCA). After all, TOGAF itself foresees (by all means) the need for its tailoring – so we are not doing anything wrong here with being “lean” or “agile”… And if we start losing the grip with some “new” VICA… Well, we will just rethink and adopt (I guess) – again…

Acknowledgment

*) Intro photo by Alex wong on Unsplash

References

[1] VUCA - Wikipedia

[2] Enterprise architecture - Wikipedia

[3] About the Zachman Framework - Zachman International - FEAC Institute

[4] DODAF - DOD Architecture Framework Version 2.02 - DOD Deputy Chief Information Officer

[5] TOGAF | www.opengroup.org

[6] Leading Through VUCA (Volatility, Uncertainty, Complexity, and Ambiguity) | by Pamela Meyer, Ph.D. | Medium

[7] what is architectural thinking | Architectural Thinking – Applied Architecture for Solutions and Enterprises

[8] SAP AppHaus • Lean Architecture Toolkit Express – Describe your minimum viable architecture

[9] SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework - SAP Community

[10] Framework - Scaled Agile Framework

[11] Sinergija: Continuous Transformation

[12] Enterprise Architecture in the era of Agile… - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

  1. Configuring the Entra ID tenant to provision users into the SAP CIS tenant requires to add "SAP Cloud Identity Services" from the Microsoft Entra application gallery to the Entra ID tenant's list of enterprise applications. This enterprise application is scoped to provision only users from the "SAP User" group, and has access to the credentials (username and password) of a system administrator with the name "Entra tenant bestruncorp" created in the SAP CIS tenant. This system administrator account has the required authorization in the SAP CIS tenant to execute the provisioning operations in the scenario. For applications listed in the gallery like "SAP Cloud Identity Services", provisioning follows the SCIM standard to ensure cross-domain interoperability. The enterprise application centrally controls all  required identity attribute mapping and transformation logic in the scenario, such as the SAP login name derived from the Windows user name, and construction of the SNC mapping following the format "p:<Windows user name>@<Kerberos realm name>". For transferring both values to CIS, the custom attributes defined in SAP's SCIM user schema extension "urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User" are used.
  2. New users in Entra are provisioned to the SAP CIS tenant's identity directory. As the persistent layer in the SAP CIS tenant, the identity directory is the source system in the tenant's IPS for the synchronization of users and groups to downstream target systems, such as the on-premise SAP system in this scenario."

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Wrap up SAP Inside Track Melbourne - 21st May 2025
entry.content:
Thank you to the SAP Community members who were able to make for the SAP Inside Track Event on 21st May 2025. 

We had an awesome turn out because of two awesome session which were

Demystifying Clean Core and the Free Clean Core Assessment Service - Paul Fewster from SAP

Empowering RISE with SAP: How Cloud ALM Enables Clean Core and Business Transformation  - Shruthi Jayaram from SAP

Thank you to the two speakers @ Paul Fewster and @Shruthi Jayaram

We would like to thank our friends at AGL Energy for hosting us and SAP Press (@sappress) for their continued sponsorship.

 

The people who attended got the opportunity to get 15% on their next SAP Press Book purchase. Please don’t forget the offer expires on 30th June 2025.

 

 

 

Looking forward to hearing from presenters for the next events.

Please register your interest below 

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/171dzmroKLzRvdVo9Sp6Ndqs0qhPZrVna24CRjMkFoQg/edit

The next planned dates are as follows

20th Aug
19th Nov

Please register your RSVP in community event page to get a badge like below 

 

Details of the next event below

https://community.sap.com/t5/melbourne-events/sap-inside-track-melbourne-20th-aug-2025/ev-p/14092612

 https://community.sap.com/t5/melbourne-events/sap-inside-track-melbourne-19th-nov-2025/ev-p/14092613

 

Cheers,

SAP Melbourne Community
